sources.title: The efficacy of semax in the treatment of patients at different stages of ischemic stroke
sources.journal: Zhurnal Nevrologii i Psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
sources.abstract: Clinical trial evaluating Semax efficacy and timing of rehabilitation on plasma BDNF levels, motor performance, and Barthel index in post-ischemic stroke patients. Semax administration increased BDNF levels, accelerated recovery, and improved motor performance.

sources.title: Investigation of mechanisms of neuro-protective effect of semax in acute period of ischemic stroke
sources.journal: Zhurnal Nevrologii i Psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
sources.abstract: Clinical investigation of neuroprotective mechanisms of Semax in acute ischemic stroke patients, demonstrating protective effects without affecting vital systems.

sources.title: Evaluation of therapeutic effect of new Russian drug semax in optic nerve disease
sources.abstract: Clinical trial evaluating Semax in vascular, toxic allergic, and inflammatory optic nerve diseases. Semax improved visual acuity, extended visual field, and protected nervous tissue.

sources.title: Semax in the treatment of glaucomatous optic neuropathy in patients with normalized ophthalmic tone
sources.abstract: Clinical trial demonstrating advantages of Semax-containing neuroprotective therapy over traditional treatment in glaucoma patients with normalized intraocular pressure.

sources.title: Functional Connectomic Approach to Studying Selank and Semax Effects
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Resting-state fMRI study comparing Semax and Selank effects on functional connectivity in healthy participants, demonstrating distinct patterns for nootropic vs. anxiolytic peptides.

sources.title: Novel Insights into the Protective Properties of ACTH(4-7)PGP (Semax) Peptide at the Transcriptome Level Following Cerebral Ischaemia-Reperfusion in Rats
sources.abstract: Genome-wide transcriptome analysis identifying 394 differentially expressed genes following ischemia-reperfusion with Semax treatment. Semax suppressed inflammatory genes and activated neurotransmission genes.

sources.title: The peptide semax affects the expression of genes related to the immune and vascular systems in rat brain focal ischemia: genome-wide transcriptional analysis
sources.abstract: Genome-wide study showing Semax predominantly enhances expression of immune system genes following focal ischemia, with largest increases in immunoglobulin and chemokine genes.

sources.title: Brain Protein Expression Profile Confirms the Protective Effect of the ACTH(4-7)PGP Peptide (Semax) in a Rat Model of Cerebral Ischemia-Reperfusion
sources.abstract: Protein expression profiling confirming neuroprotective effects of Semax in cerebral ischemia-reperfusion model, with enhanced neurotrophin transcription and immune response gene modulation.

sources.title: Semax, an analog of ACTH(4-7), regulates expression of immune response genes during ischemic brain injury in rats
sources.abstract: Study showing Semax enhances antigen presentation, intensifies interferon signaling, and affects immunoglobulin synthesis in ischemic brain injury.

sources.title: The Peptide Drug ACTH(4-7)PGP (Semax) Suppresses mRNA Transcripts Encoding Proinflammatory Mediators Induced by Reversible Ischemia of the Rat Brain
sources.abstract: Semax significantly decreases Il1a, Il1b, Il6, Ccl3, and Cxcl2 mRNAs, compensating for ischemia-induced increases. Protective effect attributed to anti-inflammatory activity.

sources.title: Comparison of the temporary dynamics of NGF and BDNF gene expression in rat hippocampus, frontal cortex, and retina under Semax action
sources.abstract: Semax administration results in rapid, long-term, specific activation of BDNF and NGF expression changes in hippocampus, frontal cortex, and retina.

sources.title: Effect of semax on the temporary dynamics of brain-derived neurotrophic factor and nerve growth factor gene expression in the rat hippocampus and frontal cortex
sources.abstract: Study of temporal dynamics of BDNF and NGF gene expression following Semax administration, showing rapid induction with 8-fold BDNF and 5-fold NGF increases at 30 minutes.

sources.title: Semax, an analog of ACTH(4-10) with cognitive effects, regulates BDNF and trkB expression in the rat hippocampus
sources.abstract: Semax affects cognitive function by modulating expression and activation of hippocampal BDNF/TrkB system. Treated animals showed increased conditioned avoidance reactions.

sources.title: Semax, an analogue of adrenocorticotropin (4-10), binds specifically and increases levels of brain-derived neurotrophic factor protein in rat basal forebrain
sources.abstract: Intranasal Semax binds specifically in basal forebrain and increases BDNF protein levels 3 hours after administration. BDNF increase observed in basal forebrain but not cerebellum.

sources.title: The heptapeptide SEMAX stimulates BDNF expression in different areas of the rat brain in vivo
sources.abstract: Semax stimulates BDNF expression in multiple brain regions including hippocampus and cortex.

sources.title: Rapid induction of neurotrophin mRNAs in rat glial cell cultures by Semax, an adrenocorticotropic hormone analog
sources.abstract: Rapid induction of BDNF and NGF mRNAs in rat glial cell cultures following Semax treatment, suggesting astrocyte-mediated mechanism of neurotrophin upregulation.

sources.title: Semax and Pro-Gly-Pro activate the transcription of neurotrophins and their receptor genes after cerebral ischemia
sources.abstract: Both Semax and PGP activate transcription of neurotrophins and their receptors in cortex following permanent middle cerebral artery occlusion.

sources.title: Semax, an ACTH(4-10) analogue with nootropic properties, activates dopaminergic and serotoninergic brain systems in rodents
sources.abstract: Semax increases striatal 5-HIAA (25-180%), potentiates amphetamine effects on dopamine release. Demonstrates modulatory effect on serotonergic system.

sources.title: Effects of Semax on dopaminergic and serotoninergic systems of the brain
sources.abstract: Study examining Semax effects on dopamine and serotonin metabolism in rodent brain systems.

sources.title: Therapeutic possibility of Semax for depression
sources.abstract: Hypothesis paper proposing Semax as potential antidepressant based on its ability to stimulate BDNF synthesis and modulate monoaminergic systems.

sources.title: Semax, an analogue of adrenocorticotropin (4-10), is a potential agent for the treatment of attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder and Rett syndrome
sources.abstract: Hypothesis paper proposing Semax for ADHD (via dopamine/BDNF effects) and Rett syndrome (via BDNF-mediated mechanisms).

sources.title: Influence of Semax on the emotional state of white rats in the norm and against the background of cholecystokinin-tetrapeptide action
sources.abstract: Semax normalized anxiety and depression induced by CCK-4, demonstrating anxiolytic and antidepressant effects under elevated anxiety/depression states.

sources.title: Semax, synthetic ACTH(4-10) analogue, attenuates behavioural and neurochemical alterations following early-life fluvoxamine exposure in white rats
sources.abstract: Semax reduced anxiety-like behaviour, improved learning abilities, and normalized brain biogenic amines impaired by early fluvoxamine exposure.

sources.title: Experimental Substantiation of Application of Semax as a Modulator of Immune Reaction on the Model of Social Stress
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Semax acted as effective immune corrector, restoring cellular and humoral immunogenesis and phagocytic activity of neutrophils in social stress model.

sources.title: Protective effect of peptide semax (ACTH(4-7)Pro-Gly-Pro) on the rat heart rate after myocardial infarction
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Semax demonstrated cardioprotective effects, protecting heart rate parameters following myocardial infarction in rat model.

sources.title: Novel synthetic analogue of ACTH 4-10 (Semax) but not glycine prevents the enhanced nitric oxide generation in cerebral cortex of rats with incomplete global ischemia
sources.abstract: Semax prevents enhanced nitric oxide generation in cerebral cortex during incomplete global ischemia, unlike glycine.

sources.title: Semax peptide targets the mu opioid receptor gene Oprm1 to promote deubiquitination and functional recovery after spinal cord injury in female mice
sources.abstract: Semax improved spinal cord injury functional recovery by targeting mu-opioid receptor, regulating USP18 and FTO deubiquitination. Research from multiple Chinese institutions.

russianApproval.vitalDrugStatus: Added to Russian List of Vital & Essential Drugs on December 7, 2011
russianApproval.indication: Ischemic stroke, dyscirculatory encephalopathy, cognitive disorders, optic nerve atrophy
russianApproval.developer: Institute of Molecular Genetics, Russian Academy of Sciences
russianApproval.note: Prescription medication in Russia and Ukraine; not approved elsewhere

developmentHistory.rationale: Extension stabilizes molecule against proteolysis and adds neuroprotective activity; removes hormonal (cortisol-stimulating) effects of parent ACTH
developmentHistory.developedBy: Institute of Molecular Genetics (IMG), Russian Academy of Sciences

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Approved in Russia (~1997); List of Vital & Essential Drugs (2011); not approved elsewhere
evidenceSummary.keyStrength: Extensive Russian clinical experience (25+ years); comprehensive genome-wide mechanistic studies; robust BDNF/NGF upregulation; low toxicity profile
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: Most research from Russian institutions; limited independent Western replication; no FDA/EMA trials; historical studies may not meet current GLP/GCP standards

institutions: Institute of Molecular Genetics, Russian Academy of Sciences
russia.focus: Development, clinical trials, mechanism studies, transcriptomics

china.focus: Spinal cord injury, mu-opioid receptor mechanisms
china.note: Recent expansion of international research base (2025)

usa.focus: Cognitive vitality assessment and literature review
